title: Pete Julius replaces the convenience store with Erwin Rommel's old armory
description:
Here's the result of a very big project that was going on November. I don't know what was inspiring me to do this. At first, I just made a video with bunch of different sources, but then I had a very interesting idea in my mind to print out every frame and then capture them and compile them into one video (except the frames that looked exactly the same as the previous or the next). I set the project framerate to 24 fps (average framerate of film) so that I didn't have to print out so many frames, because it would have taken very long on 30 fps and it already took very long. I made a photoshop file and put 3x6 frames on each page. I used different pieces of paper filled with text to print the frames on them (schoolwork stuff, announcements and lots of other trash), because I wanted to give the result a weird touch, that I call "analogue moshing" and for giving the term its full meaning, I printed on draft quality (later on normal quality, as the lines made the frames totally unrecoginzable) with a color ink that had very little tint and in the end, the printer was struggling with getting out the last tint. Not everything handwritten on the papers was written by myself. The next step was filling the white borders with color by hand. For the last step, I used a stopmotion program and a dv camera to capture and compile every frame into this movie.

Sony Vegas 6 project file: http://www.mediafire.com/?muhecflhyaip8e6
